headline: E-Cigarette Health Effects Monitoring Absent Despite 3.2 Million Users
context: Malaysia has no systematic health surveillance for e-cigarette users despite rapid growth of vaping population.

cards.big: 3.2 million Malaysians use e-cigarettes in 2025 but no health monitoring system tracks outcomes
cards.sub: Long-term health effects of vaping remain unknown for this large population

cards.big: Control of Tobacco Products for Public Health Act 2024 regulates sales but not health surveillance
cards.sub: Regulatory focus on market control missed the opportunity to mandate outcome monitoring

cards.big: US CDC's EVALI investigation detected 2,800 vaping-related lung injuries through active surveillance
cards.sub: Without monitoring Malaysia may miss emerging health signals until crisis proportions

cards.big: UK PHE conducts annual e-cigarette health review tracking respiratory and cardiovascular outcomes
cards.sub: Systematic evidence review enables evidence-based policy adjustment over time

cards.big: Regulating an industry without monitoring what it does to health is flying blind
Should MOH establish mandatory health outcome tracking for all registered e-cigarette users
